entries.title: 3.5L Petrol
entries.history: The original Z33 powerplant, aluminium V6 delivering smooth power but prone to oil starvation issues on early units.
commonFailures: Rod bearing failure from oil gallery design (NTB05-087)
Oil pump drive shaft shear under high RPM stress
Timing chain tensioner wear beyond 150k miles

entries.title: 3.5L Petrol
entries.history: Revised VQ35 with VVEL, dual intake, higher output - but infamous for paper gallery gasket failures.
commonFailures: Oil gallery gasket failure (paper gaskets disintegrate)
VVEL solenoid wear causing performance issues
Timing cover reseal required during gasket repair

commonFailures: Excessive oil consumption from piston ring wear (TSB NTB10-042)
Exhaust cam lobe wear on high-mileage units
VVEL actuator and oil rail cracks (TSB NTB08-031, NTB11-017)
Timing chain tensioner wear requiring preventative replacement

commonProblems.h2: Nissan Z Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: Oil Gallery Gasket Failure - Why VQ35HR/VQ37VHR Engines Lose Pressure
problems.rootCause: Defective paper oil gallery gaskets disintegrate under heat, dropping oil pressure and clogging pickup tubes with debris.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove timing cover, replace paper gasket with metal-reinforced OEM upgrade (13533-JK21B)
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before bearing damage; 16-22 hour job

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Gallery gasket + timing chain, tensioners, guides, water pump while cover is off
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended approach; addresses multiple wear points in one labour-intensive job

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Upgraded Gaskets
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with metal-reinforced gallery gaskets, updated rings, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage or damaged engines; includes 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: If your 350Z HR or 370Z shows low oil pressure but no knock, the gallery gasket fix with timing components is cost-effective. If rod knock is present, replacement is the only safe option. On a Z worth £3,000-£8,000, a rebuilt unit with upgraded gaskets preserves value.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt VQ35HR/VQ37VHR engine prices for your Nissan Z

problems.h4: Rod Bearing Failure - Why Early 350Z VQ35DE Engines Knock
problems.rootCause: Oil gallery design causes starvation at the #5 main bearing under high RPM or low oil conditions, documented in Nissan Service Bulletin NTB05-087.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Attempting bearing replacement without full rebuild risks immediate re-failure
repairOptions.longevity: Not viable once knock is audible; damage is usually catastrophic

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Upgraded Bearings
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt VQ35DE with upgraded rod/main bearings, revised oil pump, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Only reliable solution; rebuilt units address the root oil starvation design

problems.recommendation: If you hear a knock from your early 350Z, stop driving immediately. Rod bearing failure is catastrophic and cannot be repaired in-situ. For a clean-chassis 350Z, a rebuilt VQ35DE with upgraded bearings is the most cost-effective path forward.
problems.cta: Get quotes for VQ35DE rebuilt engines for your Nissan 350Z

problems.h4: HPFP Failure - Why New Z VR30DDTT Engines Won't Start
problems.rootCause: High-pressure fuel pump (22660-6JG0A) internal wear causes pressure loss, addressed in Nissan Recall R/2023/019 covering all RZ34 production.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Dealer replaces HPFP with revised unit under recall R/2023/019
repairOptions.longevity: First step for any no-start or performance issue on RZ34; check VIN eligibility

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace both high and low-pressure fuel pumps (16450-5TA0A) with updated parts
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if recall doesn't apply or LPFP also shows fault codes

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt VR30DDTT with Fuel System Upgrade
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt VR30 with upgraded HPFP, tensioners, bench-tested, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only option if catastrophic damage occurred; high cost reflects twin-turbo complexity

problems.recommendation: Always check recall eligibility first via Nissan UK with your VIN. If covered, the HPFP replacement is free at a main dealer. If not, a specialist can fit the updated pump at lower labour rates. Only consider full replacement if the engine has suffered secondary damage.
problems.cta: Compare VR30DDTT replacement options for your new Nissan Z

problems.h4: Oil Consumption & VVEL Wear - Why 370Z VQ37VHR Engines Burn Oil
problems.rootCause: Defective piston rings allow oil past into combustion chambers; VVEL actuator wear causes variable valve timing issues, documented in TSBs NTB10-042, NTB08-031.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - PCV + Oil Catch Can Install
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Upgrade PCV system and fit catch can to reduce oil vapour recirculation
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary mitigation only; does not fix root ring or VVEL issues

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, replace rings with updated TSB-spec parts, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable for engines with good compression but high consumption; 20+ hour job

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt VQ37VHR with Updated Rings
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with updated ring pack, VVEL components, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage 370Z; rebuilt units include all TSB updates and warranty

problems.recommendation: If your 370Z uses over 1L oil per 1,000 miles but runs smoothly, ring replacement may be viable. If compression is low or VVEL codes are present, a rebuilt unit with all TSB updates is more cost-effective long-term.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt VQ37VHR engines with updated rings for your 370Z

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Wear - Why Z Engines Rattle on Startup
problems.rootCause: OEM tensioners lose hydraulic pressure over time; VR30 units (13075-5TA0A) wear prematurely per Technical Campaign T-23-019.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace primary tensioner(s) with revised-spec units
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught early before chain stretch or guide damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, guides, tensioners, water pump, seals while front cover is off
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended preventative maintenance at 150k miles for VQ, 40k for VR30

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Upgraded Timing
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded timing components, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if rattle is accompanied by knock, loss of power, or codes

problems.recommendation: A light rattle on cold start that disappears after 2-3 seconds is often normal. Persistent rattle under load warrants immediate inspection. For VR30 owners, proactive tensioner replacement at 40k miles is cheaper than engine damage.
problems.cta: Compare timing service vs replacement costs for your Nissan Z

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Nissan Z
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're dealing with gallery gaskets on a 350Z HR, oil consumption on a 370Z, or HPFP issues on a new Z, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Nissan Z specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, bench testing, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Nationwide delivery and supply & fit options available.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Nissan Z Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Nissan Z and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

types.title: Nissan Z Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Professionally stripped, cleaned and inspected units with worn parts replaced to restore reliable performance.
types.frontDescription: Professionally stripped, cleaned and inspected units with worn parts replaced to restore reliable performance.
types.frontDisclaimer: Prices vary by engine code, mileage and supplier location.
types.backDescription: Reconditioned Z engines undergo thorough inspection with worn components like bearings, gaskets and seals replaced. Ideal for owners seeking reliable performance without full rebuild cost.
backBullets: Includes metal-reinforced gallery gaskets on VQ35HR/VQ37 units
Bench-tested before dispatch with compression report

types.title: Nissan Z Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Fully disassembled engines rebuilt to spec with upgraded internals addressing known failure points like gallery gaskets.

types.frontDisclaimer: Final price depends on variant, engine code and upgrade choices.
types.backDescription: Rebuilt Z engines are stripped to the block and rebuilt with upgraded components like metal gaskets, updated rings and revised tensioners. Best for long-term reliability.

Dyno-tested where possible with performance report

types.title: Nissan Z Used Engines

types.backDescription: Remanufactured Z engines are built to factory specifications using new pistons, bearings, gaskets and seals. Ideal for concours restorations or maximum longevity.
backBullets: New components throughout, not just worn parts replaced

items.familiesLabel: Common Nissan Z Petrol Engine Families
families: VQ35DE - Early 350Z (2003-2006), rod bearing failure risk from oil starvation

VR30DDTT - New Z (2022+), HPFP recall and timing tensioner issues

Gallery gasket and rod bearing issues on ageing VQ35 units
Oil consumption on high-mileage VQ37VHR engines
Complex but powerful twin-turbo VR30 with specialist maintenance needs
typicalModels: 370Z Enthusiast / GT / Touring / Roadster / NISMO / Track Edition
Z / Z Performance / Z Proto Spec / Z NISMO

engineYears.h2: Nissan Z Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Nissan Z's UK production history, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.description: The modern Z returns with the aluminium VQ35DE V6, launching the Z33 generation with strong sales but early oil starvation concerns.
keyChanges: Z33 350Z launched in UK, replacing Silvia-derived S-chassis
VQ35DE 3.5L V6 introduced, 276-300hp depending on market tune
Roadster (convertible) variant added to range in 2005
mainEngines: VQ35DE - 3.5L petrol (350Z base, Enthusiast, GT, Touring)
knownFor: Strong aftermarket support and tuning potential
Rod bearing failure risk on high-RPM or low-oil units (NTB05-087)
Oil pump drive shaft shear under sustained high load
Popular choice for drift and track builds due to robust chassis
years.cta: Get quotes for 2003-2006 Nissan Z replacement engines
years.ticker: 350Z engine knock • VQ35DE rod bearing failure • 350Z oil starvation fix

years.preview: Z33 Facelift: VQ35HR with VVEL Arrives
years.description: The late Z33 receives the revised VQ35HR with VVEL variable valve lift, higher output, but infamous paper gallery gasket failures.
keyChanges: VQ35HR replaces DE, adding VVEL, dual intake, higher compression
Power increased to 306-313hp with improved throttle response
NISMO edition launched with suspension and aero upgrades
Final production year for Z33 before Z34 replacement
knownFor: Smoother power delivery and sharper throttle than DE
Oil gallery gasket failure very common (paper gaskets disintegrate)
VVEL solenoid wear causing performance and noise issues
Metal-reinforced gasket upgrade (13533-JK21B) essential on rebuild
years.cta: Get quotes for 2007-2009 Nissan Z replacement engines
years.ticker: 350Z low oil pressure • gallery gasket failure • VQ35HR timing cover reseal

years.preview: Z34 370Z: VQ37VHR Becomes the UK's Most Common Z Engine
years.description: The Z34 370Z debuts with the larger 3.7L VQ37VHR, becoming the most frequently replaced Z engine in the UK due to high registration numbers and known oil consumption issues.
keyChanges: SynchroRev Match added to manual transmissions (2010+)
Facelift in 2013 with revised styling and interior updates
NISMO and Track Edition variants expand performance range
knownFor: High-revving, responsive NA character beloved by enthusiasts
Oil consumption from piston ring wear (TSB NTB10-042)
Exhaust cam lobe wear and VVEL actuator faults on high mileage
Timing chain tensioner replacement recommended at 150k miles
years.cta: Get quotes for 2009-2021 Nissan Z replacement engines
years.ticker: 370Z burning oil • VQ37VHR piston rings • 370Z VVEL noise

years.preview: Present - RZ34 New Z: VR30DDTT Twin-Turbo Era Begins
years.description: The all-new RZ34 Z debuts with the modern VR30DDTT 3.0L twin-turbo V6, delivering strong torque but introducing complex HPFP and turbo systems requiring specialist knowledge.
keyChanges: All-new RZ34 platform with retro-modern styling
VR30DDTT 3.0L twin-turbo V6, 335-420hp depending on tune
HPFP recall R/2023/019 issued for all early production units
NISMO variant launched with 420hp and chassis upgrades
mainEngines: VR30DDTT - 3.0L twin-turbo petrol (Z, Z Performance, Z NISMO)
knownFor: Strong low-end torque and modern refinement vs NA predecessors
HPFP failure (22660-6JG0A) addressed under factory recall
Timing chain tensioner wear after 40k miles (T-23-019)
Higher replacement cost reflects twin-turbo complexity
years.cta: Get quotes for 2022-present Nissan Z replacement engines
years.ticker: New Z HPFP recall • VR30DDTT no start • Z timing chain rattle

faq.h2: Nissan Z Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Nissan 350Z engine?
items.answer: Replacing a 350Z engine costs £1,500-£4,000 for a rebuilt unit depending on VQ35DE or VQ35HR code, with supply & fit adding £800-£2,000. Prices vary by supplier location and engine condition.
keyPoints: VQ35DE rebuilt: £1,500-£3,500 supply only
VQ35HR rebuilt: £2,000-£4,000 supply only
Supply & fit labour adds £800-£2,000 typically
Metal-reinforced gallery gasket essential on HR rebuilds
items.cta: Compare rebuilt 350Z engine prices for your variant

items.question: Is the Nissan 370Z VQ37VHR engine reliable?
items.answer: The VQ37VHR is generally robust but suffers documented oil consumption from piston rings and VVEL actuator wear on high-mileage units, both addressable with updated parts during rebuild.
keyPoints: Oil consumption common after 70k miles (TSB NTB10-042)
VVEL solenoid wear causes noise and performance issues
Timing chain tensioner replacement recommended at 150k miles
Rebuilt units with updated rings and VVEL components are reliable
items.cta: Find rebuilt VQ37VHR engines with TSB updates

items.question: What causes the new Nissan Z VR30DDTT to lose power or not start?
items.answer: HPFP failure is the most common cause, addressed under Nissan Recall R/2023/019. Check VIN eligibility first; if covered, replacement is free at a main dealer.
keyPoints: HPFP part 22660-6JG0A prone to internal wear
Recall R/2023/019 covers all RZ34 production
LPFP module (16450-5TA0A) can also fail independently
Timing chain tensioner wear after 40k miles per T-23-019
items.cta: Check VR30DDTT HPFP recall status for your VIN

items.question: Can I swap a VQ37VHR into my 350Z?
items.answer: Not plug-and-play. The VQ37 requires custom ECU, wiring harness, mounts and exhaust work. Most owners find a rebuilt VQ35HR more cost-effective for 350Z projects.
keyPoints: Different ECU architecture and sensor layouts
Custom engine mounts and transmission adapter required
Exhaust and cooling system modifications needed
Rebuilt VQ35HR offers similar power with simpler fitment
items.cta: Compare 350Z engine options before planning a swap

items.question: How long should a rebuilt Nissan Z engine last?
items.answer: A properly rebuilt Z engine with upgraded components addressing known failure points should last 80,000-120,000 miles with regular maintenance and quality oil changes.
keyPoints: Use metal-reinforced gallery gaskets on VQ35HR/VQ37 rebuilds
Updated piston rings essential for VQ37VHR longevity
Synthetic 5W-30 oil and 5,000-mile change intervals recommended
Monitor oil pressure and consumption post-install
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt Z engines with upgraded internals

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a high-mileage 350Z?
items.answer: For a clean-chassis 350Z with good bodywork, yes - a rebuilt VQ35 with upgraded gaskets and bearings can extend life cost-effectively. For rusted or damaged cars, consider selling.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine £1,500-£4,000 vs £3,000-£8,000 car value
Gallery gasket and bearing upgrades essential on rebuild
Verify chassis condition and rust before committing
NISMO and low-mileage examples retain value best
items.cta: Compare 350Z rebuild cost vs car value for your example

items.question: What's the difference between VQ35DE and VQ35HR engines?
items.answer: The HR adds VVEL variable valve lift, dual intake, higher compression and 306-313hp vs DE's 276-300hp, but suffers oil gallery gasket failure while DE has rod bearing risks.
keyPoints: HR has VVEL, dual intake, higher compression, more power
DE prone to rod bearing failure from oil starvation
HR prone to paper gallery gasket disintegration
Not plug-and-play interchangeable due to ECU/wiring differences
items.cta: Compare VQ35DE vs VQ35HR replacement options

items.question: Do I need a specialist to fit a Nissan Z engine?
items.answer: Yes. Z engines require VQ/VR platform expertise, VVEL calibration, ECU adaptation and proper break-in procedures that generalist garages may not provide.
keyPoints: VVEL system requires specific learning procedure post-install
ECU adaptation needed for idle and throttle calibration
Proper break-in critical for longevity of rebuilt units
Network specialists offer supply & fit with warranty backing
items.cta: Find Nissan Z engine supply & fit specialists near you

items.question: What warranty comes with a rebuilt Nissan Z engine?
items.answer: Standard is 12 months unlimited mileage on rebuilt units from EngineMarket suppliers, covering parts and labour for manufacturing defects, with extended options available.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty typical on rebuilt units
Used engines usually carry 3-6 months limited cover
Warranty excludes misuse, improper installation or lack of maintenance
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering
items.cta: Compare warranty terms across Nissan Z engine suppliers

items.question: How do I know which engine code my Nissan Z has?
items.answer: The engine code is stamped on the block near the bellhousing, or check your V5C logbook, service records, or enter your registration above for instant DVLA verification.
keyPoints: Code stamped on block: VQ35DE, VQ35HR, VQ37VHR, VR30DDTT
V5C logbook Section D.2 may list engine capacity and fuel type
Service invoices often note engine code during major work
DVLA lookup via registration gives variant and engine details
items.cta: Enter your reg to identify your exact Nissan Z engine code
